title: ferrotorch-core: nested+sparse have no GPU implementation — surfaced by phase 2.8 (#770)
description:
Surfaced by Phase 2.8 conformance dispatch (#770).

Both ferrotorch-core/src/nested.rs and ferrotorch-core/src/sparse.rs are CPU-only — every storage construction uses TensorStorage::cpu, and there are no kernel paths.

PyTorch parity expectation:
- torch.nested.nested_tensor + to_padded_tensor support CUDA tensors directly.
- torch.sparse_coo_tensor / sparse_csr_tensor / sparse_csc_tensor accept CUDA inputs.
- torch.sparse.mm uses cuSPARSE for GPU sparse matmul.
- torch._C._sparse_semi_structured_apply (NVIDIA 2:4 sparsity) uses Tensor Cores.

ferrotorch gap (rust-gpu-discipline §3 violation potential):
- NestedTensor::to_padded / from_padded — pure Vec work.
- nested_scaled_dot_product_attention — pure CPU.
- PackedNestedTensor::add/sub/mul/div/map/sum_per_component/mean_per_component — Vec iter.
- SparseTensor::spmm / coalesce / to_dense / from_dense — pure CPU.
- CooTensor / CsrTensor / CscTensor format conversions and to_dense — pure CPU.
- SemiStructuredSparseTensor::compress/decompress + sparse_matmul_24 — explicitly documented as 'reference implementation' decompress-then-dense.
- SparseGrad::apply_sgd — pure CPU.

The Phase 2.8 conformance test (ferrotorch-core/tests/conformance_nested_sparse.rs) cascade-skips its 10 GPU lane tests against this issue rather than passing CPU work as GPU coverage. When a kernel implementation lands the gpu module's note_cascade_skip calls become real GPU dispatch (mirroring conformance_reduction.rs Phase 2.2 pattern).

Recommended migration order (rust-gpu-discipline §3 — PyTorch parity is the policy):
1. SparseTensor::spmm (cuSPARSE — high ROI; the embedding backward path).
2. SparseTensor::coalesce / to_dense (basic primitives).
3. NestedTensor::to_padded (small but per-batch; common SDPA setup).
4. nested_scaled_dot_product_attention (FlashAttention-nested if available).
5. sparse_matmul_24 (cuSPARSELt 2:4 — GPU is the only place this matters).
6. CSR/CSC conversions and CscTensor::to_dense.

Until then, structured Err(GpuError::Unsupported) returns are the §3-correct shape, not silent CPU fallback.
